summary: Matched the common fighter CliffWait action by moving ft_8009A804, ftCo_CliffWait_Anim, ftCo_CliffWait_IASA, ftCo_CliffWait_Phys, ftCo_CliffWait_Coll, and helper ftCo_8009A9AC from asm/melee/ft/ft_08A4.s into new C source src/melee/ft/chara/ftCommon/ftCo_CliffWait.c. The PR added the new object to obj_files.mk, introduced CliffWait motion variables and more precise ftCommonData fields around ledge timing/cooldown, adjusted cliff-common prototypes to use ftCo_GObj, and exported several still-asm helper labels as ftCo_8009xxxx symbols so the new C and remaining assembly could link. The PR report says the new file and all six listed functions matched 100%. No review comments were present in the provided slice.
what_changed: Added src/melee/ft/chara/ftCommon/ftCo_CliffWait.c with matched C implementations for ft_8009A804, ftCo_CliffWait_Anim, ftCo_CliffWait_IASA, ftCo_CliffWait_Phys, ftCo_CliffWait_Coll, and static helper ftCo_8009A9AC.
Removed the corresponding CliffWait assembly and sdata2 constants from asm/melee/ft/ft_08A4.s, while keeping later cliff/throw-related assembly in the same file.

Converted internal assembly labels such as .L_8009AA0C, .L_8009AAFC, .L_8009AE38, .L_8009AFD4, .L_8009B170, .L_8009B518, and .L_8009B56C into global ftCo_8009xxxx symbols and updated branch targets to those names.
Added src/melee/ft/chara/ftCommon/ftCo_CliffWait.h declaring the CliffWait action callbacks and ftCo_8009AA0C.
Added ftCo_CliffWait.c.o to TEXT_O_FILES in obj_files.mk so the new C file participates in the build before the remaining ft_08A4.s object.
Updated ftCo_Init.c includes to pull in ftCo_CliffWait.h and ft/ft_08A4.h.
Added mv.co.cliffwait motion variables in src/melee/ft/chara/ftCommon/types.h with fields at fp+2340, fp+2344, and fp+2348.
Refined ftCommonData layout around offsets +488 through +49C, naming +498 as ledge_cooldown and exposing x488, x48C, x490, and x49C used by CliffWait.
Moved ft_8009A804 declaration out of ft_0877.h and into the new CliffWait header.

Changed several ftcliffcommon function signatures from generic HSD_GObj* to ftCo_GObj* and reduced ftcliffcommon.h to only expose ftCliffCommon_80081298 and ftCliffCommon_80081370; CliffCatch callback declarations were moved into ft_08A4.h.
smart_moves: The C translation preserved the exact CliffWait control flow with a RETURN_IF cascade in ftCo_CliffWait_IASA, matching the assembly sequence of trying cliff climb, attack, escape, jump/other ledge actions, then the timeout helper.

Rather than decompiling the entire large ft_08A4.s unit at once, the PR carved out only the matched CliffWait functions and promoted remaining local labels to global ftCo_8009xxxx symbols so C could call into still-assembly helpers safely.

The entry function ft_8009A804 expressed previously raw offsets as meaningful state operations: changing to ftCo_MS_CliffWait, setting x221D_b7, initializing mv.co.cliffwait, choosing timer values from p_ftCommonData based on damage percent, and applying ftColl_8007B760 with p_ftCommonData->x49C.
Small wrapper callbacks ftCo_CliffWait_Phys and ftCo_CliffWait_Coll delegate to CliffCatch physics/collision, matching the original assembly thunks without overcomplicating the C.
The PR paired function matching with the needed struct/header cleanup, which reduced raw offsets and made future ledge-action decompilation easier.
decomp_lessons: For large asm files, matching a single action can require exporting neighboring still-asm helpers with address-based names; this lets one action move to C without forcing the rest of the cluster to match immediately.
When a matched function uses fighter motion variable scratch space, add a named union member for that action instead of continuing to write raw fp+2344/fp+2348 offsets.
Common-data padding can often be split into typed fields once a matched function proves offset semantics; here CliffWait justified x488, x48C, x490, ledge_cooldown at +498, and x49C.
Use typed aliases like ftCo_GObj* in action and common-fighter code when evidence supports it, but keep names address-based for helpers whose purpose is not yet established.

Action callback files should include their own action header plus only the dependent common headers needed for matching, and obj_files.mk must place the new C object before the remaining asm object when carving out from an asm unit.

A decomp PR can improve project structure even when some helper names remain unknown: isolating a matched action into ftCo_CliffWait.c creates a reusable pattern for neighboring CliffClimb, CliffAttack, CliffEscape, and CliffJump work.
naming_conventions: Matched common-action callbacks use the ftCo_ActionName_Callback convention: ftCo_CliffWait_Anim, ftCo_CliffWait_IASA, ftCo_CliffWait_Phys, and ftCo_CliffWait_Coll.
The transition/entry function retained its address-based historical name ft_8009A804 rather than inventing a semantic name not proven by evidence.
Unidentified but callable helpers were promoted from .L_8009xxxx labels to ftCo_8009xxxx globals, preserving address identity while marking the common-fighter subsystem.
The local helper matched inside ftCo_CliffWait.c was declared static as ftCo_8009A9AC, reflecting file scope and uncertain semantics.
The new header guard used an address-oriented guard name, GALE01_09A804, consistent with address-based decomp headers in this codebase.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Converted direct branches from labels like .L_8009AAFC to symbol calls like ftCo_8009AAFC so newly compiled C and remaining assembly agreed on linkage.
Removed local sdata2 constants .L_804D86B8, .L_804D86BC, and .L_804D86C0 after the C version used NULL, 1, and typed/common constants directly.
Used a ternary expression for the p_ftCommonData timer selection based on fp->dmg.x1830_percent < p_ftCommonData->x488, matching the original compare-and-select branch.
Used bool return helpers and RETURN_IF macros to reproduce early-exit IASA behavior cleanly.
Kept simple thunk functions as direct calls to ftCo_CliffCatch_Phys and ftCo_CliffCatch_Coll, matching short assembly wrappers.
Reported match status showed 0 score out of 13000 for the file and 100.00% for all listed functions, indicating the asm-to-C extraction was verified by the project’s matching tools.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, or reviews were present in the provided PR slice, so there is no recorded human review feedback to extract.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that rename ft_8009A804 or ftCo_8009A9AC to more semantic CliffWait names.
Search for cleanup of duplicated or temporary ft_08A4.h prototypes such as repeated ftCo_8009B040, ftCo_8009B1B8, ftCo_8009B2F8, ftCo_8009B4D0, ftCo_8009B518, and ftCo_8009B56C declarations.
Look for subsequent decompilation of neighboring cliff action helpers ftCo_8009AA0C, ftCo_8009AE38, ftCo_8009AFD4, ftCo_8009B170, ftCo_8009B4D0, and ftCo_8009B518.
Search later struct work that gives semantic names to ftCommonData x488, x48C, x490, x49C and mv.co.cliffwait.x4/x8.
Check later ftcliffcommon header organization to see where CliffCatch callback declarations ultimately belong.
